step_by_step_thinking:
Iontophoresis typically uses lower concentrations of medications for treatment. Fluoride, when applied topically through this method, usually employs milder dosages to avoid potential skin irritation or systemic absorption issues. Considering these factors, option A (1%) seems like an appropriate concentration that would be both effective yet safe during administration via iontophoresis.

Answer: A
